jobs.title: Executive Director of Regional Advancement for Eastern Region.
jobs.description:
Position Details

Position Information

Posting date
06/22/2026

Closing date
07/16/2026

Open Until Filled
Yes

Position Number
1129632

Position Title
Executive Director of Regional Advancement for Eastern Region

Hiring Range Minimum
$181,100

Hiring Range Maximum
$230,000

Union Type
Not a Union Position

SEIU Level
Not an SEIU Position

FLSA Status
Exempt

Employment Category
Regular Full Time

Scheduled Months per Year
12

Scheduled Hours per Week
40

Schedule

Location of Position

This position is flexible in location with strong preference for Northeast region or Hanover, New Hampshire.

Remote Work Eligibility?
Remote eligible

Is this a term position?
No

If yes, length of term in months.
NA

Is this a grant funded position?
No

Position Purpose

The Executive Director is responsible for individually securing and stewarding restricted and unrestricted gifts from assigned individuals capable of making $500,000+ gifts in support of Dartmouth College’s institutional priorities with key alumni and parents in the region, while leading the regional team to secure and steward gifts of $100,000+ averaging ~$25M-$35M a year in restricted gifts and ~$10M-$15M a year in Annual Fund gifts Develops and executes on a regional philanthropy strategy in the region, working across multiple departments in development to coordinate a comprehensive plan for annual, leadership, and principal gifts. Works in coordination with Associate Vice President for Development ( AVP ) to implement sophisticated, integrated, efficient, and impactful presence across all Dartmouth constituents in the Eastern region. Due to the geographic location of this large region and its team, this position has a high degree of autonomy and delegated authority to act on the College’s behalf.

Description

Required Qualifications - Education and Yrs Exp
Bachelors or equivalent combination of education and experience

Required Qualifications - Skills, Knowledge And Abilities

10 years of fundraising and fundraising management experience with a successful track record as a leader, manager, and strategist in a complex organizational environment, preferably in higher education
Demonstrated experience soliciting and closing seven-figure gifts
Demonstrated experience working with senior administrators, faculty, trustees, and top volunteers and donors
Demonstrated experience working across divisions within large, complex institutions
Demonstrated experience managing a team of highly accomplished fundraisers
Demonstrated experience developing strategic plans and managing projects
Excellent relationship building skills and an ability to listen carefully as well as influence and persuade others
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with demonstrated presentation abilities
Technical computer literacy essential; familiarity with CRM’s related to advancement
Understanding and comfort with the intricacies of significant business travel
Ability to work and make decisions with a high degree of autonomy
Strong organizational skills combined with individual initiative; energy, enthusiasm, and ability to work both independently and productively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment
Demonstrated negotiation skills and an understanding of prospect-centered fund raising combined with strategic planning experience
Ability to handle sensitive and confidential matters with appropriate discretion
Resolve for success and an entrepreneurial spirit
Demonstrated integrity
Ability to analyze information and prioritize decisions

Note: Employment will be contingent upon possession of a valid U.S. or Canadian driver’s license and continued qualification as an Approved Driver under the Dartmouth College Driver Safety and Motor Vehicle Policy.

Preferred Qualifications

Department Contact for Recruitment Inquiries
Lesa Knapp

Department Contact Phone Number
603-646-1317

Department Contact for Cover Letter and Title
Lesa Knapp, Assistant Vice President for Advancement Administration

Department Contact's Phone Number
603-646-1317

Equal Opportunity Employer

Dartmouth College is an equal opportunity employer under federal law. We prohibit discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, veteran status, marital status, or any other legally protected status. Applications are welcome from all.

Background Check

Employment in this position is contingent upon consent to and successful completion of a pre-employment background check, which may include a criminal background check, reference checks, verification of work history, conduct review, and verification of any required academic credentials, licenses, and/or certifications, with results acceptable to Dartmouth College. A criminal conviction will not automatically disqualify an applicant from employment. Background check information will be used in a confidential, non-discriminatory manner consistent with state and federal law.

Is driving a vehicle (e.g. Dartmouth vehicle or off road vehicle, rental car, personal car) an essential function of this job?
Yes

Special Instructions to Applicants

Dartmouth College has a Tobacco-Free Policy. Smoking and the use of tobacco-based products (including smokeless tobacco) are prohibited in all facilities, grounds, vehicles or other areas owned, operated or occupied by Dartmouth College with no exceptions. For details, please see our policy. https://policies.dartmouth.edu/policy/tobacco-free-policy

Additional Instructions

This position has a closing date of Thursday 7/16 at 10:00am.

Please submit your application by this date and time for

your application to be fully considered for the position.

Quick Link
https://searchjobs.dartmouth.edu/postings/86242

Key Accountabilities

Description

Responsible for identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of individual prospects capable of making current-use and capital commitments of $500,000+ or more over a five-year period, manages a prospect portfolio of approximately 10-20
individual prospects. Works both independently and in concert with senior officers, Trustees, colleagues, and volunteers to implement strategies including direct solicitation of gifts. Promotes maximum involvement with Dartmouth as primary means of enhancing the likelihood of increased financial support; devises individualized involvement and team engagement strategies for all prospects using a “top-down” approach to ensure that highest rated prospects receive the most attention.

Percentage Of Time
25

Description

Leads the work of a fundraising team to attract significant gifts to Dartmouth for its funding priorities, through aligning donor interest with institutional priorities, collaborating with colleagues, and demonstrating transparency and collegiality. Supports an ambitious, strategic, and operational fundraising plan to meet short and long terms goals of the institution. Working with the Associate Vice President for Development, develops an annual and long-range plan for the region that includes a commitment to growth in the planned giving program. Establishes performance goals for the team and region and is accountable for these goals, monitors progress and devises strategies to ensure success in meeting goals. Partners with fellow fundraising leaders in principal gifts, gift planning, and professional schools to ensure collaboration between teams and meeting mutual objectives. Develops knowledge base
of
tax, investment, and legal aspects of charitable giving and articulates this knowledge clearly and effectively in discussions with prospective donors, their counsel, colleagues, and volunteers. Serves as a resource to staff on matters of prospect strategy; offers new approaches
and techniques; and seeks to provide creative solutions to complex solicitations. Relies on past experience
in the field and keeps current with evolving trends in philanthropy.

Percentage Of Time
30

Description

Develops comprehensive, strategic regional strategies for all cities within the Eastern region, including all stakeholders across the division (Presidential Initiatives and Principal Gifts, Dartmouth College Fund, Professional Schools, Marketing & Engagement, Gift Planning, Conference & Events) in the strategy and execution.

Percentage Of Time
20

Description

Articulates and implements strategies for communicating with donors; drafts and customizes institutional correspondence, proposals, and other written materials for all assigned prospects. Oversees the preparation of necessary materials, including funding proposals, briefings for senior officers and Trustees
and scheduled stewardship reports, for all assigned prospects utilizing campus resources. Recommends new activities and events to enhance engagement and giving and ensure best management of leadership and principal gift prospects.

Percentage Of Time
10

Description

Recruits, organizes, trains, motivates, and provides staff support to regional Campaign volunteers responsible for soliciting leadership gifts. Works with the AVP and other senior Development managers to refine appropriate Campaign volunteer structure.

Percentage Of Time
10

Description

Serves as an important institutional representative for the Eastern Region: responds to on-campus requests and creates opportunities for increased visibility and strengthened Eastern Region presence. Earns reputation as good citizen of and ambassador for Dartmouth through strong representation of the College to both internal and external diverse constituencies; seeks status of trusted advisor, collaborative colleague, and effective mentor. Develops collegial and productive relationships with colleagues in other College programs particularly those in the Dartmouth College Fund, Gift Planning, Principal Giving, Alumni Relations, Family Giving as well as with colleagues in the three professional schools and the offices of the Provost and Dean of Faculty.

Percentage Of Time
5

Demonstrates professionalism and collegiality through actions, interactions, and communications with others appropriate to an environment that is welcoming to all.
Performs other duties as assigned.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Higher Education

jobs.title: Program Director
jobs.description:
Looking for the next innovated, fresh ideas, cause-driven member of the team to fill our director position

Job Description

Under the direction of the Executive Director of Childcare within the expectations and policies of the YMCA of Middletown, the PD will be responsible for the day-to-day operations as it pertains to all childcare program areas, with specific concentration on Club Kid.

The position is contingent on a 30, 60, and 90-day review to maintain an exempt/salary status.

Qualifications

The PD must possess a strong understanding and general belief in the YMCA’s mission and goals and a clear understanding of how it relates to youth and family programming.
This position calls for comprehensive knowledge, experience and an understanding of age-appropriate activities and best practices for elementary age youth and before/after school childcare.
This position requires proven skills in both human and public relations.
The PD must have at least a minimum of 3 years’ experience working with youth and/or family programs in a supervisory capacity and have at least an Associate’s Degree in Elementary Education or a related field.
The PD must have the ability to understand fiscal responsibilities.

Essential Functions

The PD will be responsible for the day-to-day operations of assigned Club Kid sites and a day camp component. It is the expectation that, as it regards Club Kid, the PD will visit each of their sites weekly, both AM and PM. AM visits must be completed at least 1 time a week with the visit being at least 30 minutes long. All of your sites you are in charge of need to be visited in the am each month at least one time. PM visits need to be done 2 times a week for at least 30 minutes each visit, each site. One time a week at a PM visit an activity of your choice with the ED approval needs to be done with the PD involved. If you are at a site or in the office you are required to be there until 6pm.
When making site visit a journal entry of your site visit needs to be documented. This needs to be signed off on from the site supervisor. You will also need to sign in and out on the visitors’ log. Journals will be checked on each your scheduled ono on one meetings. It is your responsibility to bring your journal on the ED during that time.
Support these programs by hiring staff and purchasing supplies that are necessary to keep any program or activity at a high quality level and keep a consistent standard of safety.
Promote growth and provide leadership for all paid staff and volunteers.
Communicate to the ED any concerns regarding staff, participants, parents, policies, activities or programs.
Help with the process of evaluating staff and programs periodically or whenever needed.
Assist in the coordinating and conducting of any staff meetings and trainings.
Assist in departmental budget process along with satisfying projected budgetary expectations.
Integrate the YMCA Core Values into all program venues.
Assist in the creation and facilitation of training venues for Club Kid staff. Assist in the organization of any training events.
Ensure that all training and paperwork pertaining to Club Kid is current and in compliance, including licensing and permit applications.
Promote Y programs throughout the community, by being a liaison between schools, community and the Y.
Complete all aspects of hiring, including employee packets along with bi-weekly payroll and yearly evaluations. Cultivate program ideas that incorporate the Y’s Core Values.
Work alongside the ED whenever necessary.
Attend any and all meetings at the request of the Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director
Be an active supporter and participant in all our campaigns and special events.
Participate in outside events that support the community and promote public awareness of the Y.
Actively support branch needs, as “Director on Duty”, on designated nights.
PTO must be approved by the ED. If there is no PTO available you will take the time without pay and unable to make the time up.
Snack shopping at Sam's or other designated shopping stores for monthly shopping for Club kid supplies and snacks. When shopping is completed all receipts will need to be logged in the receipt book located in the ED office.
Credit cards -no credit card spending without the approval of the ED.
Intake of all Club Kid paperwork including all CBC packets for all assigned staff.
Enter all DMC for Club Kid as they come in.
In-take all Club Kid paperwork for the following school year starting in June with the exception of the draft. To include making copies, rosters etc.
Fill in for director and assistant director as needed at each camp.
Vacation Camp, participate and plan days off for schools when your schools are off. The hours worked on these days will be direct service. Hours for these day will vary and depend on amount of participants.
Flexibility is Key, your supervisor can and will change or amend this agreement anytime. Employment is not guaranteed and is based on the need of the association.
Assist as needed to work at Emergency Child locations. Understand that as your experience with camp becomes greater so will your responsibilities. Our goal is to make you a specialty director and or an assistant director at one of the camps.

Cause-Driven Leadership Competencies

High quality, value based programs for the youth and families that serve our community’s needs and the mission of our Y.
Maintaining high standards of professional ethics with regard to staff, participants and their families.
The demonstration of strong values through excellent leadership and have positive influence on colleagues and staff.
A coordinated, balance between the YMCA, local school districts and our targeted communities.
Strong, positive enhancement to youth and family programming

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Non-profit Organizations

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
The YMCA of Moore County offers an opportunity for a community-focused leader to guide a strong, stable organization with deep local roots.

Job Description

YMCA OF MOORE COUNTY

POSITION:
Chief Executive Officer

Application Deadline:
August 13, 2026

Address

1400 S. Maddox

Dumas, Texas 79029

Web Address: https://www.moorecountyymca.com

The YMCA of Moore County is an equal opportunity employer. We work to ensure that everyone has the opportunity to reach their full potential with dignity.

THE OPPORTUNITY AT THE YMCA OF MOORE COUNTY

The YMCA of Moore County offers an opportunity for a community-focused leader to guide a strong, stable organization with deep local roots. With more than $1.3 million invested in recent facility improvements and a debt-free, financially sound operation, the YMCA is well positioned for continued success.

This is an opportunity to build on momentum. The CEO will serve as the visible leader of the organization, strengthening relationships with members, staff, and community partners while ensuring programs reflect local needs.

With opportunities to expand programming and deepen engagement, the next CEO will be able to make a direct and meaningful impact. Supported by an engaged Board and committed staff team, the CEO will help ensure the YMCA remains a vital resource for individuals and families across Moore County.

Qualifications

Criteria for Candidates:

Accredited four-year college degree and/or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven record of budgetary and fiscal leadership for an organization of more than $1.4 million annually.
Minimum of seven years of senior management experience with a YMCA or comparable organization, including direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Demonstrated ability to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Ability to develop innovative programs and evaluate existing or proposed programs for financial effectiveness, community relevance, and broader engagement.
Strategic planning and fundraising experience, including annual campaigns and grant opportunities.
Current YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to obtain certification within three years.

Essential Functions

KEY SKILLS AND ABILITIES OF OUR NEXT CEO

Skills

The board believes its next CEO should be an experienced, community-minded leader with the skills and background needed to achieve excellence in the following areas:

Develop and Implement a Strategic Plan:
Work effectively with volunteers, community leaders, and key staff to create and implement a strategic plan that addresses the needs of the communities served by the Y. The plan should include clear organizational strategies, defined deliverables, and measurable goals. Key Competencies: Change Leadership, Communication & Influence, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Program/Project Management

Fiscal Management:
Use high-level financial analysis to forecast trends, support growth, and maintain financial stability. Lead the organization toward balanced or surplus operations by growing earned revenue and contributions, controlling expenses, and guiding staff leaders toward strong departmental performance. Develop and implement stewardship strategies that strengthen long-term sustainability. Key Competencies: Fiscal Management, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Developing Self & Others, Philanthropy

Improve Operating Performance:
Engage the board and appropriate staff in developing an annual tactical plan that identifies key goals and objectives and supports safe, relevant, and sustainable operations. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Innovation

Position the Y as a Vital Partner for Community Change:
Serve as an ambassador for the mission, purpose, and vision for the YMCA within the community and cultivate strong relationships with key stakeholders. Key Competencies: Communication & Influence, Engaging Community, Collaboration

Build and Lead Staff:
Ensure a talent development system is in place and executed effectively while fostering a collaborative, mission-driven culture. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Inclusion, Emotional Maturity, Change Leadership

Steward Fundraising Efforts:
Develop and implement an annual fundraising plan that strengthens the YMCA’s impact through donor engagement and community support. Key Competencies: Philanthropy, Engaging Community, Communication & Influence

About The Ymca Of Moore County

The YMCA of Moore County has served the Dumas community and surrounding area since 1960. Today, it supports more than 3,000 members and operates with an annual budget of approximately $1.4 million. The organization is financially stable and debt-free, reflecting a long-standing commitment to responsible stewardship and sustainability.

Governed by a dedicated and engaged Board of Directors, the YMCA is supported by a committed staff team and a strong culture of collaboration and service. Together, they have established the YMCA as a trusted and valued presence in the community.

In recent years, the YMCA has invested significantly in its facilities, including upgrades to wellness areas, locker rooms, and program spaces, ensuring a quality experience for members and participants.

Located adjacent to the city’s main park and across from a new elementary school, the YMCA is a convenient and welcoming place for youth, families, and individuals to participate in programs that support health, wellness, and connection.

Our Mission

To put Christian principles into practice through programs that build a healthy spirit, mind, and body for all.

Our Cause & Values

To strengthen the foundations of community and instill the values of caring, honesty, respect, and responsibility.

Our Culture

The Y Experience –
The Y Experience is built from our mission and encompasses the values and philosophy by which we serve and operate

Welcoming –
We are open to all. We are a place where you can be yourself and find yourself in others.

Genuine –
We value you and embrace your individuality.

Hopeful –
We believe in you and your potential to do good in the world.

Nurturing –
We support you as you become the best-version-of-yourself.

Determined –
Above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger, beginning with you

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Develop programs and facility offerings aligned with local needs
Strengthen partnerships and community engagement
Advance planning and construction of new gymnasium

Membership:
3,043 total members, 1,270 membership units

Core Programs

Youth Sports
Swim Lessons
Swim Team
Child Care
Group Fitness

Annual Support Campaign

Current Year Goal: $155,350
Prior Year’s Total: $110,000
Current Year’s Progress Toward Goal: $24,509
Total number of people receiving financial assistance prior year: 685

Recent Capital Campaign:
none

CEO Direct Reports

Finance/Human Resource/Membership
Aquatic Director
Program Director
Maintenance Director

Board of Directors:
10 members with the following standing committees:

Finance
Membership
Program
Facility

Financial Snapshot

Current Annual Budget Size: $1,417422
Current Debt: $0
Current Endowment: $0

About Our Community

Dumas, Texas, is a welcoming Panhandle city located about 45 miles north of Amarillo. Known for its strong civic pride and neighborly spirit, Dumas offers a family-oriented quality of life with affordable living, short commute times, open spaces, and access to regional resources.

Residents benefit from local schools, healthcare services, parks, youth sports, a public library, and a college campus, along with community events that bring people together throughout the year. The area supports an active and engaged population with a strong sense of connection.

The local economy includes agriculture, manufacturing, energy, transportation, and small businesses, providing a stable foundation for the community and the surrounding region.

Information Sites

City of Dumas: https://www.dumastx.gov/

Moore County: https://www.co.moore.tx.us/

Chamber of Commerce: https://dumaschamber.org/

Dumas ISD: https://www.dumasisd.org/

Moore County Hospital District: https://mchd.net/

Dumas Economic Development Corporation: https://www.dumas-edc.org/

Salary, Benefits, & Criteria

Salary Range:
$68,000 to $75,000 annually

Benefits

The YMCA of Moore County offers a competitive benefits package that includes participation in the YMCA Retirement Plan, a 403(b) savings plan, health insurance, paid time off, and additional benefits in accordance with YMCA personnel policies.

Residency Expectation:
Living in the YMCA’s service area is required.

Criteria For Candidates

Accredited four-year college degree and/or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven record of budgetary and fiscal leadership for an organization of more than $1.4 million annually.
Minimum of seven years of senior management experience with a YMCA or comparable organization, including direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Demonstrated ability to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Ability to develop innovative programs and evaluate existing or proposed programs for financial effectiveness, community relevance, and broader engagement.
Strategic planning and fundraising experience, including annual campaigns and grant opportunities.
Current YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to obtain certification within three years.

SEARCH PROCESS TARGET DATES

Applications accepted through: August 13, 2026
Preliminary interviews scheduled: Week of September 21
In-person interviews: October 20 and October 21, 2026
CEO selection made: Week of October 26, 2026
New CEO on board: Targeted for December 1, 2026, or as mutually agreed upon
dates subject to change

How To Apply

References, Background Check, and Organizational Leader Certification:

Candidates must submit a resume and cover letter in PDF format as part of the online application. Applications submitted without a cover letter will not be considered.

Candidates who advance to final interviews will be asked to submit six references, including four professional and two personal references. References will be checked only with prior notification. The successful candidate must pass a background check and have a current Y-USA Organizational Leader Certification or obtain certification within the first three years as CEO. The YMCA’s personnel policies may require additional screening.

Candidates Must Apply Via The Following Website

https://tinyurl.com/Y-of-Moore-County-CEO

QUESTIONS

About the Search Process?

Shea Boschee, YMCA of the USA CEO Search, [email protected], 312.419.8363

About the YMCA?

Gigi Woodruff, Texas Y Alliance, [email protected], 312.209.2993

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Non-profit Organizations

new_jobs.title: Program Director
new_jobs.description:
Looking for the next innovated, fresh ideas, cause-driven member of the team to fill our director position

Job Description

Under the direction of the Executive Director of Childcare within the expectations and policies of the YMCA of Middletown, the PD will be responsible for the day-to-day operations as it pertains to all childcare program areas, with specific concentration on Club Kid.

The position is contingent on a 30, 60, and 90-day review to maintain an exempt/salary status.

Qualifications

The PD must possess a strong understanding and general belief in the YMCA’s mission and goals and a clear understanding of how it relates to youth and family programming.
This position calls for comprehensive knowledge, experience and an understanding of age-appropriate activities and best practices for elementary age youth and before/after school childcare.
This position requires proven skills in both human and public relations.
The PD must have at least a minimum of 3 years’ experience working with youth and/or family programs in a supervisory capacity and have at least an Associate’s Degree in Elementary Education or a related field.
The PD must have the ability to understand fiscal responsibilities.

Essential Functions

The PD will be responsible for the day-to-day operations of assigned Club Kid sites and a day camp component. It is the expectation that, as it regards Club Kid, the PD will visit each of their sites weekly, both AM and PM. AM visits must be completed at least 1 time a week with the visit being at least 30 minutes long. All of your sites you are in charge of need to be visited in the am each month at least one time. PM visits need to be done 2 times a week for at least 30 minutes each visit, each site. One time a week at a PM visit an activity of your choice with the ED approval needs to be done with the PD involved. If you are at a site or in the office you are required to be there until 6pm.
When making site visit a journal entry of your site visit needs to be documented. This needs to be signed off on from the site supervisor. You will also need to sign in and out on the visitors’ log. Journals will be checked on each your scheduled ono on one meetings. It is your responsibility to bring your journal on the ED during that time.
Support these programs by hiring staff and purchasing supplies that are necessary to keep any program or activity at a high quality level and keep a consistent standard of safety.
Promote growth and provide leadership for all paid staff and volunteers.
Communicate to the ED any concerns regarding staff, participants, parents, policies, activities or programs.
Help with the process of evaluating staff and programs periodically or whenever needed.
Assist in the coordinating and conducting of any staff meetings and trainings.
Assist in departmental budget process along with satisfying projected budgetary expectations.
Integrate the YMCA Core Values into all program venues.
Assist in the creation and facilitation of training venues for Club Kid staff. Assist in the organization of any training events.
Ensure that all training and paperwork pertaining to Club Kid is current and in compliance, including licensing and permit applications.
Promote Y programs throughout the community, by being a liaison between schools, community and the Y.
Complete all aspects of hiring, including employee packets along with bi-weekly payroll and yearly evaluations. Cultivate program ideas that incorporate the Y’s Core Values.
Work alongside the ED whenever necessary.
Attend any and all meetings at the request of the Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director
Be an active supporter and participant in all our campaigns and special events.
Participate in outside events that support the community and promote public awareness of the Y.
Actively support branch needs, as “Director on Duty”, on designated nights.
PTO must be approved by the ED. If there is no PTO available you will take the time without pay and unable to make the time up.
Snack shopping at Sam's or other designated shopping stores for monthly shopping for Club kid supplies and snacks. When shopping is completed all receipts will need to be logged in the receipt book located in the ED office.
Credit cards -no credit card spending without the approval of the ED.
Intake of all Club Kid paperwork including all CBC packets for all assigned staff.
Enter all DMC for Club Kid as they come in.
In-take all Club Kid paperwork for the following school year starting in June with the exception of the draft. To include making copies, rosters etc.
Fill in for director and assistant director as needed at each camp.
Vacation Camp, participate and plan days off for schools when your schools are off. The hours worked on these days will be direct service. Hours for these day will vary and depend on amount of participants.
Flexibility is Key, your supervisor can and will change or amend this agreement anytime. Employment is not guaranteed and is based on the need of the association.
Assist as needed to work at Emergency Child locations. Understand that as your experience with camp becomes greater so will your responsibilities. Our goal is to make you a specialty director and or an assistant director at one of the camps.

Cause-Driven Leadership Competencies

High quality, value based programs for the youth and families that serve our community’s needs and the mission of our Y.
Maintaining high standards of professional ethics with regard to staff, participants and their families.
The demonstration of strong values through excellent leadership and have positive influence on colleagues and staff.
A coordinated, balance between the YMCA, local school districts and our targeted communities.
Strong, positive enhancement to youth and family programming

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Non-profit Organizations

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
The YMCA of Moore County offers an opportunity for a community-focused leader to guide a strong, stable organization with deep local roots.

Job Description

YMCA OF MOORE COUNTY

POSITION:
Chief Executive Officer

Application Deadline:
August 13, 2026

Address

1400 S. Maddox

Dumas, Texas 79029

Web Address: https://www.moorecountyymca.com

The YMCA of Moore County is an equal opportunity employer. We work to ensure that everyone has the opportunity to reach their full potential with dignity.

THE OPPORTUNITY AT THE YMCA OF MOORE COUNTY

The YMCA of Moore County offers an opportunity for a community-focused leader to guide a strong, stable organization with deep local roots. With more than $1.3 million invested in recent facility improvements and a debt-free, financially sound operation, the YMCA is well positioned for continued success.

This is an opportunity to build on momentum. The CEO will serve as the visible leader of the organization, strengthening relationships with members, staff, and community partners while ensuring programs reflect local needs.

With opportunities to expand programming and deepen engagement, the next CEO will be able to make a direct and meaningful impact. Supported by an engaged Board and committed staff team, the CEO will help ensure the YMCA remains a vital resource for individuals and families across Moore County.

Qualifications

Criteria for Candidates:

Accredited four-year college degree and/or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven record of budgetary and fiscal leadership for an organization of more than $1.4 million annually.
Minimum of seven years of senior management experience with a YMCA or comparable organization, including direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Demonstrated ability to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Ability to develop innovative programs and evaluate existing or proposed programs for financial effectiveness, community relevance, and broader engagement.
Strategic planning and fundraising experience, including annual campaigns and grant opportunities.
Current YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to obtain certification within three years.

Essential Functions

KEY SKILLS AND ABILITIES OF OUR NEXT CEO

Skills

The board believes its next CEO should be an experienced, community-minded leader with the skills and background needed to achieve excellence in the following areas:

Develop and Implement a Strategic Plan:
Work effectively with volunteers, community leaders, and key staff to create and implement a strategic plan that addresses the needs of the communities served by the Y. The plan should include clear organizational strategies, defined deliverables, and measurable goals. Key Competencies: Change Leadership, Communication & Influence, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Program/Project Management

Fiscal Management:
Use high-level financial analysis to forecast trends, support growth, and maintain financial stability. Lead the organization toward balanced or surplus operations by growing earned revenue and contributions, controlling expenses, and guiding staff leaders toward strong departmental performance. Develop and implement stewardship strategies that strengthen long-term sustainability. Key Competencies: Fiscal Management, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Developing Self & Others, Philanthropy

Improve Operating Performance:
Engage the board and appropriate staff in developing an annual tactical plan that identifies key goals and objectives and supports safe, relevant, and sustainable operations. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Innovation

Position the Y as a Vital Partner for Community Change:
Serve as an ambassador for the mission, purpose, and vision for the YMCA within the community and cultivate strong relationships with key stakeholders. Key Competencies: Communication & Influence, Engaging Community, Collaboration

Build and Lead Staff:
Ensure a talent development system is in place and executed effectively while fostering a collaborative, mission-driven culture. Key Competencies: Developing Self & Others, Inclusion, Emotional Maturity, Change Leadership

Steward Fundraising Efforts:
Develop and implement an annual fundraising plan that strengthens the YMCA’s impact through donor engagement and community support. Key Competencies: Philanthropy, Engaging Community, Communication & Influence

About The Ymca Of Moore County

The YMCA of Moore County has served the Dumas community and surrounding area since 1960. Today, it supports more than 3,000 members and operates with an annual budget of approximately $1.4 million. The organization is financially stable and debt-free, reflecting a long-standing commitment to responsible stewardship and sustainability.

Governed by a dedicated and engaged Board of Directors, the YMCA is supported by a committed staff team and a strong culture of collaboration and service. Together, they have established the YMCA as a trusted and valued presence in the community.

In recent years, the YMCA has invested significantly in its facilities, including upgrades to wellness areas, locker rooms, and program spaces, ensuring a quality experience for members and participants.

Located adjacent to the city’s main park and across from a new elementary school, the YMCA is a convenient and welcoming place for youth, families, and individuals to participate in programs that support health, wellness, and connection.

Our Mission

To put Christian principles into practice through programs that build a healthy spirit, mind, and body for all.

Our Cause & Values

To strengthen the foundations of community and instill the values of caring, honesty, respect, and responsibility.

Our Culture

The Y Experience –
The Y Experience is built from our mission and encompasses the values and philosophy by which we serve and operate

Welcoming –
We are open to all. We are a place where you can be yourself and find yourself in others.

Genuine –
We value you and embrace your individuality.

Hopeful –
We believe in you and your potential to do good in the world.

Nurturing –
We support you as you become the best-version-of-yourself.

Determined –
Above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger, beginning with you

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Develop programs and facility offerings aligned with local needs
Strengthen partnerships and community engagement
Advance planning and construction of new gymnasium

Membership:
3,043 total members, 1,270 membership units

Core Programs

Youth Sports
Swim Lessons
Swim Team
Child Care
Group Fitness

Annual Support Campaign

Current Year Goal: $155,350
Prior Year’s Total: $110,000
Current Year’s Progress Toward Goal: $24,509
Total number of people receiving financial assistance prior year: 685

Recent Capital Campaign:
none

CEO Direct Reports

Finance/Human Resource/Membership
Aquatic Director
Program Director
Maintenance Director

Board of Directors:
10 members with the following standing committees:

Finance
Membership
Program
Facility

Financial Snapshot

Current Annual Budget Size: $1,417422
Current Debt: $0
Current Endowment: $0

About Our Community

Dumas, Texas, is a welcoming Panhandle city located about 45 miles north of Amarillo. Known for its strong civic pride and neighborly spirit, Dumas offers a family-oriented quality of life with affordable living, short commute times, open spaces, and access to regional resources.

Residents benefit from local schools, healthcare services, parks, youth sports, a public library, and a college campus, along with community events that bring people together throughout the year. The area supports an active and engaged population with a strong sense of connection.

The local economy includes agriculture, manufacturing, energy, transportation, and small businesses, providing a stable foundation for the community and the surrounding region.

Information Sites

City of Dumas: https://www.dumastx.gov/

Moore County: https://www.co.moore.tx.us/

Chamber of Commerce: https://dumaschamber.org/

Dumas ISD: https://www.dumasisd.org/

Moore County Hospital District: https://mchd.net/

Dumas Economic Development Corporation: https://www.dumas-edc.org/

Salary, Benefits, & Criteria

Salary Range:
$68,000 to $75,000 annually

Benefits

The YMCA of Moore County offers a competitive benefits package that includes participation in the YMCA Retirement Plan, a 403(b) savings plan, health insurance, paid time off, and additional benefits in accordance with YMCA personnel policies.

Residency Expectation:
Living in the YMCA’s service area is required.

Criteria For Candidates

Accredited four-year college degree and/or equivalent work experience.
Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven record of budgetary and fiscal leadership for an organization of more than $1.4 million annually.
Minimum of seven years of senior management experience with a YMCA or comparable organization, including direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Demonstrated ability to develop and strengthen community relationships.
Ability to develop innovative programs and evaluate existing or proposed programs for financial effectiveness, community relevance, and broader engagement.
Strategic planning and fundraising experience, including annual campaigns and grant opportunities.
Current YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to obtain certification within three years.

SEARCH PROCESS TARGET DATES

Applications accepted through: August 13, 2026
Preliminary interviews scheduled: Week of September 21
In-person interviews: October 20 and October 21, 2026
CEO selection made: Week of October 26, 2026
New CEO on board: Targeted for December 1, 2026, or as mutually agreed upon
dates subject to change

How To Apply

References, Background Check, and Organizational Leader Certification:

Candidates must submit a resume and cover letter in PDF format as part of the online application. Applications submitted without a cover letter will not be considered.

Candidates who advance to final interviews will be asked to submit six references, including four professional and two personal references. References will be checked only with prior notification. The successful candidate must pass a background check and have a current Y-USA Organizational Leader Certification or obtain certification within the first three years as CEO. The YMCA’s personnel policies may require additional screening.

Candidates Must Apply Via The Following Website

https://tinyurl.com/Y-of-Moore-County-CEO

QUESTIONS

About the Search Process?

Shea Boschee, YMCA of the USA CEO Search, [email protected], 312.419.8363

About the YMCA?

Gigi Woodruff, Texas Y Alliance, [email protected], 312.209.2993
